item.title: Don’t You Know
item.dc:creator: Bernice Mendoza
item.description:
Laying deep within ones soul’s, sleeping desires thoughts too heavy to behold
floating above all the commotions in this world
drawing out the wisdom of all creation
built upon a single thought
fire blazing where no heat can be found
I am imagination,
seeing what has not yet been reveal
I am breath,
the first sign of life,
I cannot be seen,
yet I have always been around
I cannot be found,
yet I was never lost
hidden deep within ones soul
question me and you will find
the answers that I hold
who am I? I upon you

item.title: Remember Me in June
item.dc:creator: Bernice Mendoza
item.description:
This man of mine put it all together
our Anniversary, His Birthday,
Father’s Day too, to make it
on us as he would assume
easier during the month of June
The children wonder, the wife ponder,
how can something so important
come so quickly, to remember to
love to honor, to care, to
remember a day when a love one
camas to be. to remember a father
who’s important to us. and all the
other father out there too
doing the right thing and making it true
and a wife who’s devotion and love needs
to be esteem.

item.title: Welcome To My Blog
item.dc:creator: Tristan O'Malley
item.description:
This is the first post in a Quarto blog. Welcome!

Since this post doesn’t specify an explicit image, the first image in the post will be used in the listing page of posts.

item.title: In the Blissful Years
item.dc:creator: Bernice Mendoza
item.description:
Jump when the jumping is good
Dance when the dancing is easy
Don’t spend your years looking back
Stay always ready to dance
Sing a song, sing it lively
Don’t stop your ears
to the laughter of children
Open your eyes
See the beauty all around you
So that it don’t fade away out of sight
Make a good living when then the living is good
We can only count on what we have today
